schema.idea: Airline Load Factor Capacity Crunch Signal

data.source: BTS monthly airline load factor and capacity utilization through bts_airline_load_factor adapter

schema.behavior: When airline load factors spike above historical levels while capacity remains flat, it signals demand surge without supply expansion, lifting airline and travel sector pricing power.

market_impact.rationale: High load factors and tight capacity allow airlines to raise fares and improve margins; travel discretionary spending is strong.

trade_logic.entry: If monthly load factor exceeds 85% and is above 12-month average by 2%+ while available seat miles grow less than 1%
trade_logic.exit: Exit after 14 trading days or if load factor falls below 82% or available seat miles grow 2%+ signaling new capacity

schema.reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Seasonal travel peaks and capacity management create 2-3 high-load-factor periods per quarter.
